{"key_event":"Napoleonic centralization and imperial rule","reasoning":"The Consulate and Empire greatly expanded administrative, fiscal, legal, police, and military capacity through prefects, codification, censorship, conscription, and centralized finance. Civil society lost much of its revolutionary political autonomy through suppressed clubs, weakened legislatures, censorship, and plebiscitary personal rule, though property-owning and commercial society gained some security from legal codification and stabilization.","society_change":-2.0,"society_power":5.0,"state_change":1.5,"state_power":8.5}
